commit.message:
Add DigitalOcean CLI skill and assign to relevant agent roles (#9)

Adds a `digitalocean-cli` skill covering `doctl` and the DO REST API,
with DNS record management as the primary focus. Assigns the skill to
the three roles that have a legitimate need for it.

## New skill: `skills/digitalocean-cli/SKILL.md`

Covers the full surface area agents will need:

- **DNS** — full CRUD via `doctl compute domain records` and direct REST
API (`/v2/domains/{domain}/records`), including A, CNAME, MX, TXT
examples
- **Compute** — Droplet listing/creation/deletion
- **Managed Databases** — cluster inspection, connection strings,
firewall rules
- **Spaces** — S3-compatible object storage via `doctl` and `aws` CLI
with custom endpoint
- **Kubernetes (DOKS)** — cluster listing, kubeconfig retrieval
- **Billing** — invoice listing, account balance via REST API

## Role assignments (`openclaw.json`)

| Role | Mode | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `tech-lead` | **rw** | Owns DNS and infra operations |
| `cost-controller` | **ro** | Resource inventory and billing inspection
|
| `ceo` | **ro** | Oversight visibility |

PM and biz-research excluded — neither role operates infra directly.

## DNS record example

```bash
# Create a TXT record (e.g. SPF)
doctl compute domain records create example.com \
  --record-type TXT --record-name @ \
  --record-data "v=spf1 include:sendgrid.net ~all" --record-ttl 3600

# REST API equivalent
curl -s -X POST "https://api.digitalocean.com/v2/domains/example.com/records" \
  -H "Authorization: ******" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"type":"TXT","name":"@","data":"v=spf1 include:sendgrid.net ~all","ttl":3600}'
```

## Supporting updates

- `workspace-tech-lead/TOOLS.md` — added DO DNS workflow notes and
tagging reminder
- `workspace-cost-controller/TOOLS.md` — added DO billing commands;
updated `cloud_infra` cost category to include DigitalOcean

commit.message:
feat: parallel git clones with persistent cache (#10)

* feat: parallel git clones with persistent cache

- Add `cache_dir` param to GitManager; clones live in the persistent
  cache directory and survive between builds instead of being wiped by
  cleanup()
- Add `prefetch_all()` to GitManager: fans out clone_or_update() for all
  git repos via ThreadPoolExecutor, collecting every error before raising
  so all failures are visible at once
- Fix pre-existing bug: self.repos[name] was only set on fresh clones, not
  on updates, so extract_folder() always raised for cached repos
- cleanup() now only removes work_dir when it differs from cache_dir,
  preserving the persistent clone cache
- Add ConfigManager.get_git_cache_dir() and get_max_parallel_clones()
  with sensible defaults (.git-cache, 4 workers)
- Wire settings into PackageBuilder.__init__ and build_all_packages():
  prefetch phase runs in parallel before sequential Unity generation
- Add .git-cache/ to .gitignore
- Add tests/test_git_manager.py covering cache dir, bug fix, prefetch
  parallelism, error aggregation, and cleanup behaviour
- Add get_git_cache_dir / get_max_parallel_clones tests to
  tests/test_config_manager.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update copilot-instructions for parallel git cache feature

- Update GitManager description to mention cache_dir and prefetch_all()
- Replace stale work-dir note with accurate description of .git-cache/
  persistent cache and settings.yaml configuration keys

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

- git_manager: fast-forward branch HEAD after fetch when ref unchanged,
  so cached branches track latest remote commits
- git_manager: cleanup() safely handles cache nested inside work_dir
  using is_relative_to(); removes only non-cache entries in that case
- git_manager: sort repo names in aggregated RuntimeError message for
  stable, deterministic output
- config_manager: validate max_parallel_clones is a positive integer;
  raise ValueError with a clear message on bad config
- package_builder: skip redundant clone_or_update in _build_git_package
  when repo was already prefetched (check git_manager.repos)
- package_builder: fix _check_git_package_updates to look for repos in
  git_manager.cache_dir instead of work_dir (bug: always reported missing)
- tests: fix brittle test_max_workers_is_respected (remove broad
  try/except, configure mock executor __enter__ properly)
- tests: add cleanup nested-cache test, sorted-error-message test, and
  max_parallel_clones validation tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add PackageBuilder.build_all_packages unit tests

Cover the parallel-prefetch path introduced in the parallel git cache
feature. Nine test cases exercise:

- prefetch_all called only for git packages (not NuGet)
- max_workers from config forwarded to prefetch_all
- build_package called for every configured package
- correct return value (list of built paths)
- NuGet-only config skips prefetch_all entirely
- empty package list returns []
- build_package exception propagates
- prefetch url/ref/name values match packages.yaml
- _build_git_package skips clone_or_update when repo already prefetched

package_builder.py coverage: 66% → 75%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ewa): UX redesign grounded in cognitive science research

Psychology-driven UX changes across 5 phases, 16 items:

Phase A — Card Intelligence:
- 'Why now' urgency rationale + expected outcome on gallery cards
- Three-state confidence (Ready to send/Review suggested/Draft only)
- Composite sort (urgency×confidence) + 5-item focused queue cap
- Draft ownership framing ('Your draft', cursor mid-text, learning feedback)

Phase B — Attention Protection:
- Quiet mode toggle in FilterBar (filters Medium priority)
- Enhanced StatusBar with day metrics (decisions, focus, drafts, meetings)
- Visual state transitions (completion slide-out, status pulse animations)

Phase C — Briefing Variants:
- Morning briefing: start-of-day summary with first decision + focus window
- End-of-day review: completed/deferred/focus stats + tomorrow carry-forward
- Enhanced meeting briefing: what changed, open decisions, talking points

Phase D — Contextual Intelligence:
- Context-aware CommandBar prompts (action-oriented chips)
- Agent activity feed (timestamped side effects in CommandBar)
- New DayGlance component (today's calendar with prep status)

Phase E — Visual Sustainability:
- Warm-gray palette (#fafaf8 base, reduces blue tint for 8-hour use)
- Animation budget (prefers-reduced-motion, finite memoryPulse)
- Typography sustainability (14-15px body, 1.5 line-height, single bold tier)

Research basis: Cowan (4±1 attention), Gloria Mark (23-min interruption cost),
Zeigarnik (unfinished task tension), arXiv 2024 (AI trust miscalibration),
PMC visual fatigue, BJ Fogg behavior model, Hick's Law.

Build passes, 213 tests pass (16 suites).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(adversarial): raise L1 pass threshold to 8 when agent wrote real files

When an agent used code_write/code_edit (actual work), soft quality
issues like Overclaim or Contradiction on the description text (not
HALLUCINATION/SLOP/MOCK/LIES/ECHO/FAKE) should not block the cycle.
Raise threshold from 6 to 8 in that case.

Rationale: ac-architect writes INCEPTION.md (documentation), the L1
reviewer scores 7/10 for 'Overclaim' (agent claims file has all
elements, reviewer can't verify because code_read wasn't called).
This fix unblocks cycles that do real work but have imprecise text.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 7.1: AUTO-DETECTION & FALLBACK

Implement automatic GPU detection with software rendering fallback per
ROADMAP Phase 7.1. All three rendering paths (Intel GPU, AMD GPU, Software)
now accessible via unified interface with automatic selection.

Selection logic:
1. If ForceSoftware flag set → use software renderer
2. Attempt GPU detection at /dev/dri/renderD128
3. If Intel GPU (Gen9-Xe) detected → BackendIntelGPU
4. If AMD GPU (RDNA1-3) detected → BackendAMDGPU
5. If no GPU or init fails → BackendSoftware fallback

Implementation:
- Created Renderer interface for unified backend API
  * Render() and RenderWithDamage() for display list execution
  * Present() for DMA-BUF export (GPU) or error (software)
  * Dimensions() and Destroy() for resource management

- Implemented NewRenderer() auto-detection function
  * Queries GPU via render.DetectGPU()
  * Maps GPU generation to backend type
  * Creates appropriate backend or falls back to software
  * Supports ForceSoftware override for testing

- Created SoftwareBackend wrapper
  * Wraps SoftwareConsumer for unified interface
  * Implements damage tracking for incremental updates
  * Returns -1 from Present() (software has no DMA-BUF)
  * Provides Buffer() method for direct pixel access

- Added auto-render-demo binary
  * Demonstrates all three rendering paths
  * Tests auto-detection and fallback logic
  * Supports FORCE_SOFTWARE=1 environment override

Testing:
- All 21 new backend tests passing (100% pass rate)
  * Interface contract validation
  * Auto-detection with various GPU types
  * Software backend functionality
  * Invalid dimension handling
- All existing tests still passing
- Static linking verified (ldd: "not a dynamic executable")

Metrics:
- 8 complexity improvements vs 4 regressions (improving trend)
- Quality score: 38.1/100
- Zero critical issues in new code
- Regressions in unrelated files (x11-dmabuf-demo, wayland/shm)
